title: Akamai Guardicore Breach Detection Time-to-Containment Metrics (`guardicore:incident`)
spl:
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:incident" earliest=-30d
| where isnotnull(detected_time) AND isnotnull(contained_time)
| eval ttc_sec=contained_time-detected_time
| stats median(ttc_sec) as med_ttc p90(ttc_sec) as p90_ttc by incident_type
description: Board-level metrics require measuring how fast segmentation limits damage.
value: Security leadership measures Mean Time to Contain and Mean Time to Resolve for microsegmentation-detected breaches, providing board-level metrics that demonstrate security program maturity and justify continued investment.
implementation: Install the add-on, configure API polling intervals appropriate to API limits, and map assets to CMDB for enrichment lookups.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count

references.title: Splunkbase — Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk

knownFalsePositives: Microseg blocks from new app deployments, missing exception rules, or planned segmentation rollouts.
grandmaExplanation: We measure how quickly our team spots and stops a security breach inside the network, tracking those times over months to show our defenses are getting faster and better.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Akamai Guardicore Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7426) v3.x collecting incident data from the Centra REST API.
• Data in `index=microseg` with `sourcetype=guardicore:incident`. Key fields: `incident_id`, `severity`, `detected_time`, `contained_time` (when the affected asset was isolated or the policy was enforced), `resolved_time`, `incident_type`, `affected_assets`, `containment_action` (isolate/policy_enforce/manual).
• At least 30 days of incident data for meaningful metrics. 90 days provides quarterly trending.
• This UC produces board-level metrics: Mean Time to Detect (MTTD), Mean Time to Contain (MTTC), and Mean Time to Resolve (MTTR). These are key indicators of security program maturity.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify incident lifecycle fields:
```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:incident" earliest=-30d
| where isnotnull(detected_time) AND isnotnull(contained_time)
| stats count by severity
```
If `contained_time` is null for most incidents: analysts may not be using the containment workflow in Centra. This is a process issue, not a data issue.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Time-to-containment metrics:
```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:incident" earliest=-90d
| where isnotnull(detected_time) AND isnotnull(contained_time)
| eval ttc_minutes=round((contained_time - detected_time)/60, 0)
| eval ttr_minutes=if(isnotnull(resolved_time), round((resolved_time - detected_time)/60, 0), null())
| eval ttc_band=case(ttc_minutes<=15, "<15 min", ttc_minutes<=60, "15-60 min", ttc_minutes<=240, "1-4 hours", ttc_minutes<=1440, "4-24 hours", 1==1, ">24 hours")
| stats count avg(ttc_minutes) as avg_ttc median(ttc_minutes) as median_ttc p90(ttc_minutes) as p90_ttc avg(ttr_minutes) as avg_ttr by severity
| eval avg_ttc=round(avg_ttc, 0) | eval median_ttc=round(median_ttc, 0) | eval p90_ttc=round(p90_ttc, 0) | eval avg_ttr=round(avg_ttr, 0)
```

Understanding this SPL: Calculates containment and resolution times per severity. The median is more meaningful than the average because a single multi-day incident can skew the mean. The p90 (90th percentile) represents the worst 10% of incidents — if this is hours for critical incidents, the containment process needs improvement. Target: critical incidents should be contained within 15 minutes (the 'golden hour' of breach response).

Monthly trending:
```spl
index=microseg sourcetype="guardicore:incident" earliest=-90d
| where isnotnull(detected_time) AND isnotnull(contained_time)
| eval ttc_minutes=round((contained_time - detected_time)/60, 0)
| eval month=strftime(detected_time, "%Y-%m")
| stats median(ttc_minutes) as median_ttc count as incidents by month, severity
| sort month, severity
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In Guardicore Centra: Incidents > select a resolved incident > verify the detected, contained, and resolved timestamps match Splunk.
(b) Check for data quality: incidents where `contained_time < detected_time` indicate a clock sync issue or incorrect field mapping. These should be excluded.
(c) Compare metrics with your SIEM/SOAR ticketing system for cross-validation.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard ("Guardicore — Breach Metrics"):
• Row 1 — Single-value tiles: "Median TTC (critical)", "Median TTR (all)", "Incidents (90d)".
• Row 2 — Bar chart: TTC distribution bands by severity.
• Row 3 — Timechart: monthly median TTC trending (should decrease over time).
• Row 4 — Table: individual incidents with TTC, TTR, severity, containment action.

Report: Monthly executive summary to CISO. Quarterly board report comparing metrics against industry benchmarks.

Runbook:
1. **TTC increasing for critical incidents** — Review the SOC workflow. Are analysts receiving alerts promptly? Is the Centra isolation feature being used, or are analysts manually configuring firewall rules (slower)?
2. **TTR much longer than TTC** — Containment is fast but resolution is slow. This usually means root cause analysis or remediation is bottlenecked. Check staffing and tooling.
3. **Seasonal patterns** — TTC may be longer during holidays or off-hours. Consider automated containment (Centra can auto-isolate on critical incidents) to maintain response times 24/7.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **`contained_time` always null** — Analysts are not using the containment workflow in Centra. Train them to click 'Contain' when they isolate an asset, not just close the incident.
• **TTC shows negative values** — Clock skew between the Centra server and the agent's detected_time. Fix NTP on the Centra management appliance.
• **Very few incidents with complete lifecycle data** — The metric is unreliable with small sample sizes. Report confidence intervals or wait for more data.
